title: Daily Market Digest: June 1, 2026
subtitle: Tech Rally Continues Amid Geopolitical Tensions; Fed Cautions on Inflation Response

items.headline: Tech Stocks Propel Market Near Record Highs as Oil Prices Fluctuate
items.summary: U.S. equities saw mixed movements on Monday, with the tech-heavy Nasdaq and S&P 500 drifting higher, largely fueled by a continued rally in artificial intelligence (AI) stocks. Hopes for an imminent U.S.-Iran peace deal faded somewhat during the day, leading to a rise in oil prices that pressured oil-linked cyclicals and caused the Dow to dip. Overall market sentiment remains resilient, despite ongoing geopolitical uncertainties and inflation concerns.

items.headline: Nvidia Surges on New AI Chip Announcement
items.summary: Nvidia jumped more than 4% today after unveiling a new AI-focused PC chip, boosting mega-cap tech stocks and easing concerns about potential delays in its AI roadmap.

items.headline: Dell Technologies Continues Ascent on AI Demand
items.summary: Dell Technologies gained 8% today, following a 32.8% surge on Friday, driven by strong earnings powered by robust AI demand and a raised full-year guidance.

items.headline: Science Applications International Corp. Rises on Earnings Beat
items.summary: Science Applications International Corp. surged 12.3% after reporting quarterly profit that exceeded analysts' expectations and raising its financial outlook.

items.headline: Qualcomm Slips on PC Competition Fears
items.summary: Qualcomm fell by 7.55% today amidst fears of tougher competition in the PC market.

items.headline: United Airlines Down as Oil Prices Rise
items.summary: United Airlines fell 2.7% as oil prices moved higher following renewed tensions around the U.S.-Iran ceasefire, impacting companies with significant fuel costs.

items.headline: P&G Declines in Today's Session
items.summary: Procter & Gamble saw a 2.27% decline today.

items.headline: Technology and Energy Lead While Consumer Staples Lag
items.summary: Information Technology continued its strong performance, leading sectors today with a 1.67% gain, driven by AI optimism. The Energy sector also saw a 2.5% turnaround, propelled by rising gas prices. In contrast, Consumer Staples were among the worst performers, with the Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR (XLP) losing 2%, and Financials and Health Care also showing weakness.

items.headline: Former Fed Chair Powell Defends Independence; Governor Bowman Cautions Against Rate Hikes
items.summary: Former Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, in one of his first public appearances since leaving office, used an award speech to defend the independence of institutions like the Fed from political pressure. Meanwhile, Fed Governor Michelle Bowman warned against raising interest rates in response to the current inflation spike, particularly if it's driven by temporary energy price inflation rather than broad-based pressures. Markets are generally anticipating the Fed to keep interest rates unchanged in the near term.

items.headline: Key Economic Data Releases Expected This Week
items.summary: This week's economic calendar includes several important data releases. Monday features the ISM Manufacturing PMI, ISM Manufacturing Employment, ISM Manufacturing Prices, and Construction Spending MoM. The Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey (JOLTS) is due Tuesday, followed by the ADP Non-Farm Employment Change on Wednesday. The week concludes with the crucial BLS Employment Report on Friday, with expectations for 95,000 jobs added and a 4.3% unemployment rate.

items.headline: Major Earnings Reports Continue; Dell Shines
items.summary: Earnings season continues to be a market driver, with Dell Technologies Inc. ($DELL) notably surging 32.8% on Friday due to strong first-quarter fiscal 2027 non-GAAP earnings and robust AI demand. This week, investors anticipate reports from several large tech and cybersecurity companies, including Broadcom ($AVGO), CrowdStrike ($CRWD), Palo Alto Networks ($PANW), and Hewlett Packard Enterprise ($HPE).
